items.title: Match the structure to the access pattern
items.avoid: Choosing list reflexively for every collection.
items.prefer: list for ordered, indexable data; set for membership; dict for key→value; deque for double-ended queues.
items.why: Picking the right structure changes whole-program complexity from O(n²) to O(n).
items.example:
```python
seen = set()             # not a list
for doc in docs:
    if doc.id in seen:    # O(1) instead of O(n)
        continue
    seen.add(doc.id)
```

items.title: Build strings with join, not +=
items.avoid: Concatenating to a string inside a loop.
items.prefer: Append to a list, then `''.join(parts)` once.
items.why: Strings are immutable; joining once is O(n), `+=` in a loop is O(n²).
items.example:
```python
parts = [chunk for chunk in stream()]
text = ''.join(parts)
```

items.title: Iterate without mutating
items.avoid: Removing items from a list mid-iteration.
items.prefer: Build a filtered list with a comprehension.
items.why: Avoids silently skipped elements and index shifts.
items.example:
```python
clean = [x for x in raw if is_valid(x)]
```

items.title: Vectorize numeric work
items.avoid: Manual Python loops over float lists for embedding math.
items.why: C-level loops with packed data are dramatically faster and use less memory.
items.example:
```python
import numpy as np
sims = embeddings @ query        # (n, d) @ (d,) -> (n,) in C
top_k = np.argpartition(-sims, 5)[:5]
```
